commit.message:
Add mutualism study analysis and game-specific instructions

New files:
- instructions_mutualism.md: game parameters, cross-benefit payoff structure,
  data format, figure panel mapping
- mutualism.md: full analysis of partner choice (P) at given=1.0

Key findings:
- Cross-benefit payoffs: each population's R-P equals the partner's b-c
- Pop_0 (lower b-c) cooperates in 97% of off-diagonal cells because it
  receives larger benefits from its partner
- Partner choice bottleneck: cooperation scales with min(b_c_0, b_c_1)
  because swaps require C1P1 on both sides
- Diagonal recovers Hamilton's stochastic symmetry breaking
- Genotype composition and exploitation patterns match Hamilton

fix(sf2): replace broken after-process callback with reliable setInterval polling

The after-process callback on _producerNode.onaudioprocess was not firing
because the ScriptProcessorNode is created lazily during first play, but
the callback was installed during engine init before the node exists.
With no setInterval fallback, position updates stopped entirely.

Fix: go back to simple setInterval(8ms) polling of sequenceIndex from
C64 RAM. 125Hz polling is fast enough to catch every row change. The
explicit rowDuration from currentSeqEventDuration provides consistent
visual timing via FormatPlaybackState smooth interpolation.

commit.message:
Wire up CustomTargetLanguage and force MachineTranslate

Add EffectiveTargetLanguage computed property to Configuration that
returns CustomTargetLanguage when UseCustomLanguage is active, else
SelectedTargetLanguage. Replace all runtime reads of
SelectedTargetLanguage with EffectiveTargetLanguage in
TranslationHandler, PhraseFilter, and LanguageDetector.

When custom language is active, bypass the engine switch in
TranslateMessage and route directly to MachineTranslate (Bing/Google),
since DeepL and LLM engines may not support arbitrary languages.

Update AGENTS.md to document EffectiveTargetLanguage, MachineTranslate
forcing, and IsTargetIsoCode limitation for custom languages.

feat: AI-proof editing — ast.parse() gate, rollback, structured errors

Pre-write validation: _validate_python_syntax() runs ast.parse() on new
content before _write_and_cache() writes to disk. Broken Python never
reaches the filesystem.

Structured error output: _parse_error_for_agent() converts SyntaxError
into actionable instructions with line numbers, context window, and
caret markers — the LLM can self-correct without re-reading the file.

Rollback on failure: _rollback_edit() restores .bak backup and
invalidates _step_file_cache when validation fails. No half-written
files left behind.

Create file guard: _act_create_file() validates syntax before writing
new .py files, preventing creation of files with broken syntax.

Add dashboard UI with real-time active song syncing via useSyncedState (#12)

Adds a TV-friendly dashboard page (`/:token/dashboard`) for controlling
which song is currently playing, with real-time sync to all connected
vote pages via rwsdk's `useSyncedState`.

### Infrastructure
- Added `SyncedStateServer` Durable Object binding and v2 migration in
`wrangler.jsonc`
- Registered `syncedStateRoutes` and exported `SyncedStateServer` from
`worker.tsx`

### Dashboard (`/:token/dashboard`)
- Server component loads songs; client component (`DashboardControls`)
renders play/pause buttons per song
- State scoped to game ID via third parameter: `useSyncedState<string |
null>(null, "activeSong", gameId)`
- "Now playing" banner always reserves vertical space — shows dimmed "No
song currently playing" placeholder when idle, preventing layout shifts
- Accessible `aria-label`/`aria-pressed` on all buttons

### Vote page active song highlight
- `useActiveSong(gameId)` custom hook subscribes to the synced state key
- `VoteSongList` client component uses the hook and conditionally
applies Tailwind CSS `before:` pseudo-element classes
(`before:bg-linear-to-br before:from-indigo-500/35
before:via-purple-500/25 before:to-indigo-500/15`) for a translucent
indigo→purple gradient ring on the active song
- Custom pulsing animation defined in `styles.css` as a CSS custom
property (`--animate-active-song-pulse`), applied via
`animate-(--animate-active-song-pulse)`
- `aria-live="polite"` region announces the currently playing song for
assistive tech

### Screenshots

**Dashboard (no song playing)**
![Dashboard no
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79f2d7c4-9f2a-4d38-9d00-d2c7a3e3c6ea)

**Vote page (active song highlighted)**
![Vote active
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/975b3e0b-d1d5-460f-8fb9-4aefda37219b)

### E2E tests (3 new, 19 total passing)
- Dashboard rendering and song selection/deselection
- Cross-page real-time sync: two browser pages open simultaneously,
clicking a song on the dashboard asserts that
`[data-testid="now-playing"]` updates on the vote page without refresh

```tsx
// Dashboard sets the active song scoped to this game
const [activeSong, setActiveSong] = useSyncedState<string | null>(null, "activeSong", gameId);

// Vote page subscribes via custom hook — updates arrive via WebSocket
const activeSong = useActiveSong(gameId);
```

* Fix noisy audio: rebuild Windows DLL with libvgmstream_setup PCM16 output

Previous commit bb7ea19 fixed Linux .so but missed the Windows .dll.
The user was running the old Windows DLL which output float32 (4 bytes/sample),
causing noise when Godot's AudioStreamWAV FORMAT_16_BITS tried to interpret
float bytes as int16.

Both binaries now rebuilt with libvgmstream_setup(force_sfmt=PCM16):
- libgodot_rocksmith.so: 5,376,000 bytes decoded (PCM16 confirmed, was 10,752,000 float32)
- godot_rocksmith.dll: rebuilt with same fix (was never updated after bb7ea19)

Verified on Linux sandbox:
  AudioEngine: decoded 496786 WEM bytes → 5376000 PCM bytes (2 ch, 48000 Hz)
  = 28s × 48000 × 2ch × 2 bytes (PCM16) ✓

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/santzit/go-guitar2/sessions/5adfc328-5224-4897-9f4c-d867421d6d9d

security+fix: address all deep review findings (1 HIGH + 5 MEDIUM)

HIGH:
- Confluence env var whitelist: only allow reading from approved
  vars (prevents leaking arbitrary env vars through config)

MEDIUM:
- Atomic file writes in hitl-server.py via os.rename()
- Port exhaustion: exit with error if all 8900-8999 occupied
- Server PID verification after startup with cleanup on failure
- Python3/gh/curl dependency checks in all adapter scripts
- Confluence HTML injection: strip raw HTML from pandoc input

Also removed dead code in local-html.sh (static HTML processing).

commit.message:
Integrate single-run analysis findings into interpretation docs

- Add README contents table and mention 1run study variants
- Document hamilton_1run and prisoners_1run in instructions files
- Integrate 1run temporal dynamics findings into prisoners.md:
  - Phase boundary: low-cooperation values are genuine equilibria, not cycling
  - Pop_2 asymmetry: absorbing state, stochastic role assignment
  - Pop_3 tipping: single early jump, no cycling
  - C1P0 displacement: oscillatory coexistence, never completes
- Integrate 1run findings into hamilton.md:
  - Gradual transition confirmed genuine, not averaging artifact
  - Pop_2 roles lock in early; weaker correlation from low-b-c noise
  - Pop_3 jump: one-time tipping event
  - C1P1/C1P0 anti-phase oscillation at frequency-dependent equilibrium
